behaviorEssentials: Resolve presentation using deterministic cascade precedence: defaults, then matching selectors in document order, then item-specific overrides.
Apply token references in style and widget configuration at theme-application time; unresolved tokens should fall back to renderer defaults with diagnostics.
Treat platform as an open string hint; unknown platform values should not block theme application.
When preferred widgets are unavailable, apply declared fallback chains before reverting to renderer-native defaults.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming theme document must include $formspecTheme=1.0, version, and targetDefinition.
Renderers must not let theme content alter core data semantics, validation semantics, or response payload shape.
Implementations should validate targetDefinition compatibility ranges and warn on mismatch rather than hard-failing rendering.

behaviorEssentials: Execute rules in deterministic order after defaults and direction resolution; higher priority executes first and same-priority rules preserve document order.
Evaluate condition guards before transform application; skipped rules must not write target values.
Support explicit reverse overrides and bidirectional flags to control reverse execution behavior.
Treat adapters as serialization boundaries: mapping evaluates against structured data before format-specific output encoding.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming mapping document must include version, definitionRef, definitionVersion, targetSchema, and at least one rule.
Processors must reject invalid mapping documents and unknown transform declarations rather than silently ignoring them.
Reverse execution through non-reversible transforms must be rejected or explicitly disabled by rule configuration.

behaviorEssentials: Response documents are bound to an exact definition identity tuple and must be interpreted against that pinned version.
Response status transitions are business-process concerns, but completed responses must satisfy error-level validation expectations.
Validation findings can be carried inline through validationResults without changing the canonical response data payload.
Optional metadata fields (author, subject, id) should not alter structural validity of required response content.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming response must include definitionUrl, definitionVersion, status, data, and authored.
Processors must reject responses that do not conform to the response schema shape.
Pinned definition references must not be silently substituted with different versions during processing.

behaviorEssentials: ValidationReport aggregates all findings at a point in time and should be treated as an immutable evaluation snapshot.
The valid flag must be derived from error-level findings, not from warning or info counts.
Counts must remain consistent with results so report summaries and downstream decisions stay deterministic.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming validation report must include valid, results, counts, and timestamp.
ValidationReport results entries must conform to the shared validationResult.schema.json schema.
Processors should reject reports where required severity counts are missing or invalid.

behaviorEssentials: Definition documents declare structure, logic hooks, and extension points but do not execute behavior by themselves.
Top-level lifecycle fields (status, version, versionAlgorithm) determine publication and compatibility semantics across versions.
Definition-level defaults and options (for example formPresentation and nonRelevantBehavior) establish baseline processing and rendering behavior.
Behavioral logic remains declarative through binds and shapes and is evaluated by conformant processors at runtime.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming definition must include $formspec, url, version, status, title, and items.
Processors must reject definitions that violate structural constraints or unsupported $formspec versions.
The (url, version) pair must be treated as immutable identity for definition pinning and response validation.

behaviorEssentials: Registry documents describe extension metadata and compatibility, but do not prescribe centralized hosting or governance.
Entry lifecycle states should guide consumer behavior for adoption, warning surfaces, and retirement handling.
Naming conventions and uniqueness constraints prevent collision and support cross-organization interoperability.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming registry document must include $formspecRegistry, publisher, published, and entries.
Registry entry names must follow x-prefixed naming rules and category-specific requirements.
Processors should reject malformed registries and conflicting duplicate name/version entry pairs.

behaviorEssentials: Changelog entries represent atomic diffs between two definition versions and should be processed in document order.
Impact classification should be derived deterministically from change semantics to drive semver governance.
Migration hints are advisory mappings that assist generation of formal migration artifacts.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming changelog document must include definitionUrl, fromVersion, toVersion, semverImpact, and changes.
Change objects must include type, target, path, and impact, with optional before/after details as applicable.
semverImpact must reflect the maximum impact level represented in the change set.

behaviorEssentials: Component documents define a single-root presentation tree where child order is rendered deterministically.
Slot binding links input components to definition item keys while preserving core behavioral semantics from the definition.
Tier 3 components take precedence over theme and inline hints for layout/widget selection, with fallback rendering for unbound items.
Core processors must substitute progressive components with declared fallbacks while preserving structure and applicable base props.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming component document must include $formspecComponent, version, targetDefinition, and tree.
Processors must reject unsupported component-document versions and unknown non-x top-level properties.
Renderable trees must satisfy component-category constraints, bind compatibility, and declared structural limits.

behaviorEssentials: Locale Documents are display-only sidecars — they MUST NOT affect data collection, validation logic, or behavioral semantics.
Resolve localized strings using a deterministic cascade: regional locale → base language locale → inline Definition defaults.
String keys use dot-delimited paths (itemKey.property) to target specific localizable properties; unmatched keys are silently ignored.
FEL interpolation via {{expression}} syntax is evaluated at render time against current form state; broken expressions fall back to the raw template string with a diagnostic.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming locale document must include $formspecLocale=1.0, version, locale, targetDefinition, and strings.
Processors MUST perform case-insensitive BCP 47 tag comparison and SHOULD normalize to lowercase language with title-case region.
Processors MUST detect circular fallback chains and terminate the cascade with a warning rather than looping.

behaviorEssentials: References Documents are standalone sidecars that bind external resources to Definition items by path — they MUST NOT alter data capture, validation, or the processing model.
Each Bound Reference targets a specific item path (or '#' for form-level); references do not inherit from parent groups to children.
The type and rel properties are open strings for forward compatibility — processors SHOULD warn on unrecognized values but MUST NOT reject the document.
Multiple References Documents may target the same Definition; their references are merged additively with document-internal order preserved.
conformanceEssentials: A conforming references document must include $formspecReferences=1.0, version, targetDefinition, and references.
Extended processors must verify targetDefinition.url matches the loaded Definition and resolve all $ref pointers at load time.
Extended processors must validate referenceDefs id-key matching and should warn on target paths that don't correspond to definition items.
